name: conceptv-design-system
description: ConceptV Design System — the sanctioned import surface (index.js) in front of the compiled bundle + the four single-source-of-truth registries (CV_REGISTRY, CV_AI, CV_AXES, CV_NODE/CV_ACTIONS/CV_HOST). See CLAUDE.md for the constitution.
_peerDependencies_note: React/ReactDOM are NOT bundled or npm-installed here — this DS's compiled bundle (_ds_bundle.js, see CLAUDE.md §5) consumes React as a runtime GLOBAL (window.React / window.ReactDOM), the same way every consumer surface already loads it via <script> before the bundle. peerDependencies above documents the version expectation for tooling (npm ls, lockfile resolution) without adding a real dependency edge — there is nothing to `npm install` for React here; provide the global at the page level.
_type_field_note: Deliberately no top-level "type" field. index.js uses ES `export` syntax (required so a browser <script type="module"> can `import` it directly — the real consumption path; browsers ignore package.json entirely). This directory ALSO holds many pre-existing CommonJS scripts (_demo/verify_*.js, _system/emit_glyph_corpus.js) that use require(). Setting "type":"module" here would break every one of them; setting "type":"commonjs" explicitly disables Node's ES-module auto-detection and makes `import('./index.js')` fail outright. Leaving "type" unset lets Node's content-sniffing fallback load index.js as ESM (emits a one-time harmless MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON perf warning under Node — irrelevant in the browser) while every existing require()-based script keeps working unmodified. Verified empirically: see _demo/verify_entry.js.
